titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 1, no. 45: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 45. (A.R. No. 99 of 1929.) LALGUDI, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Saptarīshīśvara temple.

Records the endowment of thirty kaḻañju of gold for the purpose of paying the remuneration of 2 brāhmaṇas reciting tiruppadiyam on all the three twilight periods daily by Śivakōsari Piḍāraṉ Dēśaviṭaṅkaṉ, a priest of temple (ittaḷippaṭṭuḍaiyāṉ) of Mahādēva of Tiruttavattuṟai, a dēvadāna village in Iḍaiyāṟṟu-nāḍu. Against this gold Kiḍāmbil Ichchuvara Naḍuppaya kramavittaṉ of Kuṇdina-gōtrā (Kauṇdinya-gōtra) of the dēvadāna-brahmadēya village, Anuttiramaṅgalam sold (set apart) two mā of his share-land in two plots (to be retained and administered by him) and agreed to contribute thirty nāḻi of oil per year for the stated purpose.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . matiraikoṇṭa koppara
kecaripaṉmaṟki yāṇṭu 3 10 7 Āvatu
Iṭaiyāṟṟu nāṭṭu tevatānam tiru
ttavattuṟai mahādevaṟku Itta
ḷippaṭṭuṭaiyāṉ civakocari piṭāraṉ
tecaviṭaṅkaṉ cantrādittavaṟ nicata muṉ
ṟusandhiyum brāhmaṇar tiruppatiyam pāṭu
vāriruvataṟku vaiytta poṉ 3 10 2 m Ippoṉmu
ppattiru kaḻañcum Ivvūr tevatāna piramateyamanuttirama
ṅkalattu kuṇṭina kottirattu kaṭāmpiccuvara naṭuppayakramama
vittaneṉ koṇṭu Iddevaṟku tiruppatiyam pāṭuvāriruvaṟ
May be restored as pāṭuvāriruvaṟkumākaka viṟṟu kuṭutta nilam Eṅkaḷ pitThe first two letters in the word pitrukkaḷ are found engraved like pṛ which tempts one to read as gurukkaḷrukkaḷ śāsanabaddhampa
ttuṭaiya bhumiyil Ippoṉnukku viṟṟa nilattukku
ḻ Ellai Iddevar nilattukku mekkum kāviti nilattuk
kum talaivāy nilattukku vaṭakkum Ippiṭāraṉ tecaviṭaṅ
kanukku nāṉ viṟṟa nilattukku kiḻakkayyaṭi Akalam teṉ
maṭal Oḻukkaikkuk kiḻakkum kāviri niroṭu kālukku teṟ
kkum Ivvicaitta perunāṉkellaiyil Akappaṭṭa nila muṇ
ṇilam Oḻiviṉṟi Iññilattil nāṉuṭaiyatellāmakappaṭa vi
ṟṟu Iññilattāl nānaṭṭak kaṭava Eṇṇai Āṇṭu varai patiṉ
ññāḻiyum Ittiruppatiyam pāṭuvāre Aṭṭuvārāka Ippoṉ 3 10 2m
koṇṭu Iññilam vilaikkaṟa viṟṟu kuṭutteṉ cuvara naṭuppaya kramavit
taneṉ Iññilattāl Ittiruppatiyam pāṭuvārkkāka Ivveṇṇai
3 10- yum Ippiṭāraṉ tecaviṭaṅkaneṉ nāṉ Iccuvaranaṭuppaya kra
mavittaniṭai nāṉ vilaikoṇṭa mekkaṭaiyaṉta pāti nilattiluma
ṭṭak kaṭava 3 10 yumāka 3 10 2yu māṇṭu varai nāṉ koṇṭa ṉilattile tirumāvukkuIt is evident that the engraver in the first instance had forgotten to incise this line but later on to accommodate the line he inserted it in small letters, after the whole matter was engraved.
Ivveṇṇai Eṟṟi koṇṭu candrātittavaṟ Aṭṭuvenāka tiruppatiyam pā
mMay be restored as pāṭumiruvarukkumceytu kuṭutteṉ piṭāraṉ tecaviṭaṅkaneṉ Itu
panmāheśvarat tiruvaṭi Uṭaiya Irakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 32, part 1, no. 45 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
